# codex-autoupdate installation and use

Install this project only on macOS for the currently logged-in user. It manages `/Applications/ChatGPT.app`, so first verify that ChatGPT Desktop is installed and that the user is an administrator with write access to `/Applications`.

## Install the official release

1. Verify the prerequisites:

   ```sh
   test "$(uname -s)" = Darwin
   test -d /Applications/ChatGPT.app
   id -Gn | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx admin
   command -v go
   ```

2. Install the versioned command with Go:

   ```sh
   go install github.com/tylergannon/codex-autoupdate/cmd/codex-autoupdate@v0.1.0
   ```

3. Resolve the installed binary and retain this shell variable for the remaining commands:

   ```sh
   binary=$(command -v codex-autoupdate || true)
   if [ -z "$binary" ]; then
     go_bin=$(go env GOBIN)
     if [ -z "$go_bin" ]; then
       go_bin="$(go env GOPATH)/bin"
     fi
     binary="$go_bin/codex-autoupdate"
   fi
   test -x "$binary"
   ```

4. Inspect the current state without changing anything:

   ```sh
   "$binary" check --json
   ```

5. Install and start the per-user LaunchAgent:

   ```sh
   "$binary" install
   "$binary" status
   ```

The command copies itself into `~/Library/Application Support/codex-autoupdate/`, writes `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.tylergannon.codex-autoupdate.plist`, and starts it in the current GUI login session. Logs are under `~/Library/Logs/codex-autoupdate/`.

## Configure

Global options go before the subcommand. Options passed to `install` are persisted in the LaunchAgent. The defaults require five uninterrupted idle minutes and check for updates every fifteen minutes.

```sh
"$binary" \
  --idle-window 10m \
  --poll-interval 30m \
  install
```

Useful commands:

```sh
"$binary" check
"$binary" check --json
"$binary" run --once
"$binary" status
"$binary" uninstall
```

## Behavior and recovery

The watcher compares `CFBundleVersion` with OpenAI's stable ChatGPT Desktop Sparkle appcast. It stages the full update, verifies the exact build, bundle identifier, OpenAI signing team, strict code signature, Gatekeeper acceptance, and host architecture, then waits until Desktop Codex rollout records show no active task for the configured window. It checks activity again before asking ChatGPT to quit normally. It never force-kills the app.

If the replacement app-server does not become ready, the previous app is restored and relaunched. The failed build is quarantined at `~/Library/Caches/codex-autoupdate/failed-build-BUILD.json`, preventing repeated restarts for that build. A newer build is still eligible. Remove that marker only when deliberately retrying the same build.

For a real end-to-end update proof, follow `ephemeral/projects/codex-autoupdate/human-proof.md` from a separate Terminal when OpenAI's appcast advertises a build newer than the installed build.
